channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: ESPN broadcaster reveals why he was brought to tears on broadcast after Ohio State won national championship
item.description: Kirk Herbstreit addressed his tears that were seen on the ESPN broadcast after Ohio State won the national title, when he revealed his wife, Ally, was diagnosed with breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian Open: American player Ben Shelton criticizes TV interviewers at tournament
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — American tennis player Ben Shelton criticized on Wednesday some of the questions and comments made by post-match TV interviewers at the Australian Open.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ichiro Suzuki, CC Sabathia and Billy Wagner elected to Baseball Hall of Fame
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Used to leading off, Ichiro Suzuki got antsy when he had to wait. Considered a no-doubt pick for baseball's Hall of Fame and possibly the second unanimous selection, he waited by the phone for the expected call Tuesday. Fifteen minutes passed without a ring.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: High school basketball player jumps into action to save opponent's life after harrowing on-court collapse
item.description: A high school basketball player in Oklahoma was transported to a hospital after collapsing during the opening moments of a game earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With Ben Johnson on the move to Chicago, 5 head coaching vacancies remain
item.description: Ben Johnson made a quick decision. Aaron Glenn could be next. With Johnson heading to Chicago and Mike Vrabel already in New England, five head coaching vacancies remain. The Jets, Saints, Jaguars, Raiders and Cowboys are still in the hunt.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Young collector nabs rare Paul Skenes card that could offer him a hefty haul in trade with Pirates
item.description: An 11-year-old collector in Los Angeles has scooped up a one-of-a-kind baseball card featuring National League Rookie of the Year Paul Skenes. Topps announced Tuesday that the card, which features the Pittsburgh Pirates pitcher’s autograph and a patch from a game-worn jersey, had been found.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vikings and coach Kevin O'Connell agree to a multiyear contract extension after a 14-win season
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings and coach Kevin O’Connell have agreed to terms on a multiyear contract extension.
item.dc:creator: Dave Campbell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Los Angeles 11-year-old pulls one-of-a-kind baseball card
item.description: An 11-year-old baseball card collector from Los Angeles just pulled what is likely the most important card they will ever pull. Topps, the card manufacturer, confirmed Tuesday morning that the Paul Skenes 1/1 Debut Patch card was in fact unpacked. The card features the patch that the 2023 No.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio State backup QB Devin Brown transfers to Cal
item.description: Brown, who has two years of eligibility remaining, will compete with freshman Jaron-Keawe Sagapolutele for the Bears' starting quarterback job.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Greg Norman says he would win a debate with Tiger Woods and Rory McIlroy on LIV making them richer
item.description: Greg Norman says he would win a debate with Tiger Woods and Rory McIlroy on how Saudi-funded LIV Golf has helped them financially. And by the sound of his interview with Australian Golf Digest, it wasn’t just LIV Golf where the Shark left his teeth marks.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tom McVie, longtime NHL coach, dead at 89
item.description: Tom McVie, Boston Bruins ambassador and former coach for the Washington Capitals, New Jersey Devils and Winnipeg Jets, is dead at age of 89.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coco Gauff says she has more work to do after losing at the Australian Open. Djokovic beats Alcaraz
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Coco Gauff's retooled forehand and serve abandoned her in the worst way and at the worst time at the Australian Open.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah Hockey Club at Rice Eccles Stadium? It could happen
item.description: Could Utah Hockey Club play a game outdoors at Rice Eccles Stadium? Owner Ryan Smith indicated it could be a possibility sometime in the future.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Almost a dozen Chicagoland natives are on the rosters for the teams playing in Monday's CFP Championship Game
item.description: Nearly a dozen Chicagoland natives will have a chance to win a College Football Playoff championship on Monday night in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: CMU Softball ready to chase another dominant season in 2025
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— After a record-breaking 2024 campaign that ended in postseason disappointment, the Colorado Mesa University softball team is gearing up for another dominant season.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: Run, Eagles, run: Massive plays on the ground power Eagles to NFC Championship Game
item.description: These Philly Snow Birds had cause for celebration -- they will host the NFC championship game Sunday against Washington, after the Commanders upset No. 1 seed Detroit on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Texans' Kris Boyd flings helmet, gets penalized and then shoves his coach on first play vs. Chiefs
item.description: Texans cornerback Kris Boyd nearly shoved Houston special teams coach Frank Ross to the ground after the opening kickoff of their divisional playoff loss to the Kansas City Chiefs on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs fans banking on three-peat during Wild Card Weekend
item.description: The NFL playoffs kicked off on Saturday, but the Kansas City Chiefs have to wait another week for their playoff campaign to start. It’s leaving fans with mixed feelings.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: Several Chicagoland AMC locations to air the College Football Playoff championship game
item.description: CHICAGO — Notre Dame will face off against Ohio State on Monday in the College Football Playoff championship game in Atlanta, and while traveling to the game may not be an option for some, fans in Chicago can still get a taste of the larger-than-life action at some AMC theaters.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Girls Basketball: Rancho Buena Vista 56, La Costa Canyon 51
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)-Girls Basketball on Wednesday evening part of girls and boys doubleheader for league action. Camille Dake, Senior from RBV lead the way for the Longhorns. Great game that ended with the home team winning 56-51.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Aggies six-game winning streak ends at UNLV, 65-62
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - Utah State basketball team suffered just its second loss of the season Wednesday night, falling at UNLV, 65-62. Tucker Anderson led the Aggies with 19 points, but the Aggies missed its last six shots of the game, and dropped to 16-2 on the season. Dedan Thomas Jr.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Keyonte George returns, but Jazz lose to Hornets, 117-112
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Down three starters, the Utah Jazz young players are getting a chance to shine these days. After playing very well for three quarters, the Jazz ran out of gas in the fourth and lost to the Charlotte Hornets Wednesday night, 117-112.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
